reasoning: The Lean statement precisely captures the informal claim. The hypothesis `[Group G] [Fintype G]` with `card G = 312` matches "group of order 312". The conclusion `∃ (p : ℕ) (P : Sylow p G), p.Prime ∧ (p ∣ card G) ∧ P.Normal` matches "has a normal Sylow p-subgroup for some prime p dividing its order" - the existence of a prime p dividing |G|, a Sylow p-subgroup P of G, and P being normal. In Mathlib, `Sylow p G` is the type of maximal p-subgroups and `P.Normal` asserts normality in G. The requirement `p ∣ card G` is essential since `Sylow p G` is defined for any natural p, and must be included to avoid trivializing the claim via non-dividing primes. The quantifier structure, types, and conclusions all align with the informal reading.
